candidates.why: Represents only insurance segment revenue, not total revenue for this banking group. Using it as total revenue would materially understate consolidated revenue.

Explicit balancing line tied to total revenue; while some years show -1/nan, it conceptually represents total revenue more accurately than segment-only 'Insurance revenue'. No better total-revenue candidate is available.

variables.notes: Chosen as the only candidate that conceptually corresponds to total consolidated revenue, despite being a balancing line.
No COGS/cost of sales-type row in candidate list; treated as 0 per instructions.

candidates.why: This is a net income line, not an overhead expense; inappropriate for SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Operating expenses on properties and premises
candidates.why: Clearly an operating overhead expense (property-related), fits SG&A definition and is not a total/subtotal.
Core SG&A personnel expense line, clearly overhead and not COGS or D&A; appears populated and is a primary SG&A component.
Balance sheet liability, not a period expense line, so not appropriate as SG&A expense component.

final_choice.row_label: Operating expenses on properties and premises

variables.notes: Selected main operating overhead expense components; excluded net income and balance-sheet-only items.

candidates.why: An impairment of capitalised development costs, not the recurring R&D expense; less directly representative of R&D than explicit amortization of capitalized R&D.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Explicitly labeled as amortization of capitalized R&D; best available proxy for R&D expense in the period.
Balance sheet gross capitalized development asset, not a period expense.
Accumulated amortization/impairment on balance sheet, not the current-period expense line.
Balancing adjustment line, not a clean expense measure.

final_choice.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental

variables.notes: Used explicit amortization of capitalized R&D as R&D expense proxy.

candidates.row_label: Interest on amounts due from credit institutions
candidates.why: Interest income, not expense; excluded for XINT which should reflect interest expense.
Interest income from customers; not an expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest on impaired loans and guarantees
candidates.why: Appears to be interest income on impaired exposures; values mostly nan; not clearly an expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest on commercial paper and bonds at fair value
candidates.why: Likely interest income on assets at fair value; not an expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest on commercial paper and bonds, held to maturity
candidates.why: Interest income on held-to-maturity bonds; not expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest on amounts due to credit institutions
candidates.why: Clearly an interest expense component on funding from credit institutions (negative amounts).
Key interest expense component on customer deposits (negative amounts).
Major interest expense component on issued debt securities (negative amounts).
Interest expense component on subordinated debt (negative amounts).
Appears to be other interest/dividend income, not expense.
Residual interest expense line; clearly part of interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Other Interest expense positive and net
candidates.why: Label suggests an interest expense measure, but appears unused (nan) and less clear than specific negative expense components.
Represents profit allocation, not interest.
Represents minority interest in profit, not interest expense.
Too vague and appears unused (nan); no numeric preview to anchor; less reliable than concrete components.
Ambiguous and appears unused (nan); no numeric data, unclear definition versus clear component lines.
Appears to be miscellaneous interest income; not expense.
Balance sheet minority equity, not interest expense.
Same as above: equity, not interest.

final_choice.row_label: Interest on amounts due to credit institutions

variables.notes: Mapped XINT to the main explicit interest expense components (all negative), excluding interest income and equity-related items.

candidates.row_label: Profit from investments accounted for by the equity method
candidates.why: Income statement line, not a stock measure of equity.
Appears to be an OCI item, not total book equity.
Dividend income, not equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Investments accounted for by the equity method
candidates.why: Asset representing investments in associates; not total shareholders’ equity.
Component of equity but not total book equity; excludes share capital and some reserves.
Subcomponent of equity; not total equity.
Represents total equity attributable to owners (before adding non-controlling interests), which is the appropriate book equity measure for PROF.
Includes non-controlling interests (slightly higher than Total Equity), whereas PROF BE should typically exclude minorities.

variables.notes: Chose equity attributable to owners, excluding non-controlling interests, as book equity.

candidates.why: Income statement share of profit for minorities; useful but balance-sheet minority interest is a more direct MIB stock measure.
Appears to relate to income allocation, not the balance-sheet minority equity amount.
Balance sheet line for minority equity interest; best representation of MIB.
Duplicate concept/amount to Non-controlling interests; either would work, but using one avoids double counting.

variables.notes: Mapped MIB to the balance sheet non-controlling interests (minority equity).
